channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Erik Prince: Private sector can help with mass deportations
item.description: "It was a memo generated to describe how to achieve the logistics necessary to move the millions of people that they intend to deport," Prince said.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: US energy secretary touts nuclear power as tech sector's thirst for electricity grows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright says it’s critical that the nation be out in front when it comes to artificial intelligence, and that means having reliable and affordable sources of electricity to meet the growing demands of the technology sector.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP pushes 'big' budget resolution to passage, a crucial step toward delivering Trump's agenda
item.description: With a push from President Donald Trump, House Republicans sent a GOP budget blueprint to passage Tuesday, a step toward delivering his “big, beautiful bill” with $4.5 trillion in tax breaks and $2 trillion in spending cuts despite a wall of opposition from Democrats and discomfort among Republicans
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House GOP adopts Trump budget after topsy-turvy night
item.description: House Republicans adopted the budget resolution that will lay the foundation for enacting President Trump's legislative agenda Tuesday night, just minutes after they initially pulled the measure from the floor.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Adnan Syed's murder conviction still stands as he seeks sentence reduction in 'Serial' case
item.description: Baltimore prosecutors have announced their decision to withdraw a motion to vacate Adnan Syed's murder conviction even as they support his recent request for a sentence reduction.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wu-Tang Clan announces Wu-Tang Forever: The Final Chamber Tour
item.description: The Wu-Tang Clan is hitting the road for the final time. The iconic East Coast hip-hop group announced their farewell tour called Wu-Tang Forever: The Final Chamber.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Utah bill banning water fluoridation one step away from becoming law
item.description: Fluoridation, or water fluoridation, is the action of raising naturally occurring levels of fluoride in water systems to aid in oral health of those who drink it.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of beauty products for Black women contain health hazards
item.description: The study found 80% of more than 4,000 beauty products marketed to Black women have hazardous chemicals.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Woman injured on Harry Potter theme park ride awarded $7.25 million
item.description: A federal jury has awarded $7.25 million to a 74-year-old Arizona woman for a spine injury she suffered in a fall while exiting the Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey ride at Universal Studios Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 people killed by IED; cartel suspected: Officials
item.description: A Texas rancher was killed by an explosive device in an area of Mexico where authorities warned of cartel activities.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Nashville hot chicken up for official state food in Tennessee legislature
item.description: Nashville hot chicken traces its origins back to the 1930s, and popularity of the dish has grown to international acclaim.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, NewsNation

item.title: Over 20 staffers in office overtaken by DOGE resign
item.description: The staffers said they won’t use their technical skills to “jeopardize” Americans’ data and “compromise” essential government systems.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Missouri woman accused in plot to sell Graceland says she'll plead guilty
item.description: A Missouri woman said Tuesday she is pleading guilty to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Social Security Administration closes Office of Transformation
item.description: The Office of Transformation, according to its website, is responsible for “strategic guidance and oversight of enterprise-wide initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: United Airlines flight forced to make emergency landing at Newark
item.description: A United Airlines flight carrying nearly 200 passengers made an emergency landing at Newark Liberty International Airport after a "possible mechanical issue" mid-flight, the airline said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massive blue city migrant shelter opens despite residents' furious opposition
item.description: A massive migrant shelter has opened in the Bronx despite residents venting their vehement opposition to the move.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court orders new trial for Oklahoma death row inmate
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday sided with an Oklahoma death row inmate who claimed alongside the state itself that his trial was unfair, ordering a new trial and staving off his execution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt 180 as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York inmate dies in prison as guards continue strike deemed illegal under state law
item.description: An inmate died in a New York prison as guards at prisons across the state continue their strike, which union officials acknowledge violates a state law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Mother of Palestinian American boy slain in suburban Chicago hate crime testifies at trial
item.description: Prosecutors say a suburban Chicago landlord took a knife from a belt holder and attacked a Palestinian American woman before fatally stabbing her young son 26 times in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump backs Musk as he roils the federal workforce with demands and threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump backed Elon Musk's demand that federal employees explain their recent accomplishments by the end of Monday or risk getting fired, even as government agency officials were told that compliance with Musk's edict was voluntary.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court declines to take up challenge to Tennessee law restricting drag shows
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to hear a challenge to a Tennessee law restricting some drag performances, allowing the first-in-the-nation law to remain largely intact.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mississippi city drops lawsuit over newspaper editorial that judge ordered removed
item.description: A Mississippi city dropped its lawsuit Monday against a newspaper that had its editorial criticizing local leaders removed by a judge in a case that sparked widespread outrage from First Amendment advocates.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump expresses hope Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he meets with France's Macron
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump expressed hope that Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he met Monday with French President Emmanuel Macron on the third anniversary of the invasion.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, MATTHEW LEE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Brazilian illegal alien charged with rape caught in Massachusetts, handed over to ICE in rare case
item.description: An illegal immigrant from Brazil, who entered the U.S. illegally in April 2024, was arrested last month in Falmouth, Massachusetts, and charged with rape.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Funeral set for an officer killed while responding to a gunman in a Pennsylvania hospital
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A funeral service will be held later this week for a Pennsylvania police officer shot and killed while responding to a hospital intensive care unit where authorities said a gunman had shot three hospital employees and taken staff members hostage last weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 3 boaters stuck at sea for 9 hours after their boat capsizes
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Coast Guard crews rescued three boaters in Captiva Island, Florida, after their boat flipped over and they were stuck at sea for nine hours. A family member reported concerns at 1 a.m. Sunday after not hearing from the three boaters since 8 p.m. Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minimum wage workers would have to work 106 hours to afford average apartment, report finds
item.description: A new report found that a minimum-wage employee would have to work 106 hours a week to afford a median-priced apartment in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, WGN 9

item.title: Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro meets with family of fallen officer, victims of hospital shooting
item.description: "I am inspired by the amazing doctors, nurses, and staff at UPMC and WellSpan Health who did tremendous work to save lives in the face of tragedy," said Shapiro. "It was my honor to meet with them and to thank them for what they do in service to their neighbors.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday. It marked a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, ABC 4

item.title: Venezuelan family in US under curtailed humanitarian protections clings to faith amid uncertainty
item.description: HOPKINS, Minn. (AP) — Every Sunday, Johann Teran goes to worship at a Lutheran service in suburban Minneapolis, trying to find some hope that the future he was building isn’t all slipping away.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4
